one_time_description: ISC revenue significantly higher than budget ($37.7M actual vs $20.2M budget) due to prior year deferred revenue recognition and additional ISC funding; also $7.997M interim financing from First Nations Financing Authority with no repayment terms
economic_notes: GBEs include Z'gamok Enterprises Inc., Z'gamok Construction Limited Partnership, and Robinson Huron Forestry Company Inc. Partnerships include Great Lakes Hospitality Group (11.78% interest) and Aki-eh Dibiniwewziwin Limited Partnership (16.95% interest, mining and development). SDC and Ritchie Falls Resort consolidated. ZC LP revenues $18.17M in 2023.
notes: Fiscal year is 2023 (year ended March 31, 2023). Net debt position of $948,000. ISC revenue of $37.7M significantly exceeded prior year $20.3M and budget of $20.2M due to large deferred revenue drawdowns and prior year unearned funding receivable of $2.389M. $7.997M interim financing from First Nations Financing Authority bearing 6% interest with no repayment terms treated as long-term debt. Two consolidated subsidiaries: Sagamok Development Corporation and 2344827 Ontario Inc. (Ritchie Falls Resort). Three GBEs accounted for by modified equity method. No schedule of remuneration included in provided text. Housing and social services expenses not separately broken out in segmented disclosure - included within other segments. Total government transfers calculated as all identified government funding lines excluding Other revenue and partnership income.
